'''Harry Newman''' (born 19 February 2000) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He has played for the [[England Knights]]<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=24 October 2019}}</ref> and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has spent time on [[Rugby League Dual registration|dual registration]] from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Newman was born in [[Huddersfield]], West Yorkshire, England.{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
In 2017, he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref name="Super League: Wigan Warriors 34-0 Leeds Rhinos">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40644462|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 34-0 Leeds Rhinos|publisher=BBC|date=21 July 2017|accessdate=22 July 2017}}</ref>

In 2020, Newman was named the Super League Young Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Daly |first=Phil |date=2020-11-23 |title=Newman makes history as he picks up Young Player of the Year award |url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2020/11/23/newman-makes-history-as-he-picks-up-young-player-of-the-year-award/ |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=Leeds Rhinos |language=en}}</ref>
Newman missed out on Leeds' 2020 [[2020 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] victory to [[Salford Red Devils]] and the [[2022 Super League Grand Final|2022 Grand Final]] defeat to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] due to injury.

In 2022, he made seven appearances for the Leeds club, scoring four tries.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Custom Match List |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MzQtMjE0Ni0xMywyOCwyMiwyMSwyMCwyNiwxOCwxNyw5LDE5LDUyLDUzLDIzLS0tLS0tMjM5MDgtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t |access-date=2023-03-09 |website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>
In round 12 of the 2023 Super League season, Newman scored two intercept tries for Leeds in their 40-18 victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65545806|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 18-40 Leeds Rhinos - 12-man visitors forge stunning comeback|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Newman played a total of 17 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs. Newman played 22 games for Leeds in the 2024 Super League season as the club once again finished 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
Newman played every game for Leeds in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table. Newman played in Leeds heart breaking 16-14 elimination play off game against St Helens where Leeds conceded two tries in the last five minutes to lose 16-14, the last of which came after the full-time siren. Following the game, cameras showed St Helens player [[Tristan Sailor]] approach Newman in order to console Newman, however Newman was seen telling Sailor to "Fuck Off".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce86r25lrxno/|title=Saints snatch stunning play-off victory at Leeds|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

On 2 December 2025, Newman signed a contract to join the [[Perth Bears]] ahead of the 2027 NRL season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2025-12-02/perth-bears-signing-toby-sexton-harry-newman/106090798/|title=Perth Bears sign halfback Toby Sexton and England international Harry Newman for 2027 NRL season|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>

===Hull F.C. (loan)===
After two rounds of the [[2026 Super League season|2026 season]] having not been selected in Leeds' first team, 14 April 2026, Newman was signed by injury-stricken [[Hull F.C.]] on a short-term loan ahead of a fixture against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]. Newman's signing was to cover for F.C.'s first team centre [[Arthur Romano (rugby league)|Arthur Romano]], who suffered an ankle injury in the Good Friday [[Hull derby]] fixture against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 3 April and was ruled out of the first team for up to three months.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Tomlinson |first1=Daniel |title=Hull FC sign Leeds Rhinos centre Harry Newman amid injury crisis |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-sign-leeds-rhinos-10914663 |access-date=14 April 2026 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=14 April 2026}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2019, Newman was selected for the [[England Knights]] against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref> He made his senior England debut against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the first test of the [[2023 Tonga rugby league tour of England|2023 series]] on 22 October 2023.<ref>{{cite web |title=England v Tonga: Mikey Lewis & Harry Newman set for international debuts |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67169984 |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=22 October 2023 |date=20 October 2023}}</ref>
